title: Skopeo Fails to Copy Signature from one server to another
body:
## Use Case:
1. **Sign** a Docker image in a **DevOps local registry** using **Cosign**.
2. **Create** an **OCI tar file** using **Skopeo**.
3. **Transfer** the tar file to a **remote server**.
4. **Load** the image into the **local registry** running on the **remote server** (offline server) using **Skopeo**.

### **Issue:**
I have executed the following commands successfully, **without any errors**, but the **signature is not loading into the destination server** when checked using **curl** commands.

#### **From Source Image Registry:**
```bash
curl -X GET 'http://localhost:5000/v2/my-centos-2/tags/list'
{"name":"my-centos-2","tags":["1.0","sha256-eb4e282ef535e75f86245bedb32f51766b1b04d117a3f41cd63b33702ba2f362.sig","2.0","3.0","1.1"]}
```

#### **From Destination Image Registry:**
```bash
curl -X GET 'http://localhost:5000/v2/my-centos-2/tags/list'
curl -X GET 'http://localhost:5000/v2/my-centos-3/tags/list'
{"name":"my-centos-3","tags":["1.0","1.1"]}
```

### **Commands Executed:**

### **Create an OCI tar file using Skopeo:**

```bash
skopeo copy --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/my-centos-2:1.0 oci:/root/sa/sk/out
skopeo copy --tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/my-centos-2:1.0 oci-archive:/root/sa/sk/out/1.tar
```

### **Load to destination registry using Skopeo:**
```bash
skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false oci:/root/sa/sk/out docker://localhost:5000/my-centos-2:1.1
skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false oci-archive:/root/sa/sk/out/1.tar docker://localhost:5000/my-centos-3:1.1
```

```
Skopeo Version : 1.18.0
Cosign Version: v2.4.2
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Reading sigstore signatures from registries (or writing them) requires an opt-in, https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.d.5.md#individual-configuration-sections .

… After that, copying to `oci:` or `oci-archive:` would fail because those transports don’t currently implement that. That’s not helpful short-term, I’m afraid, but it would at least not be silent.

Using `dir:` to transport the image along with signatures should work, for closely-related versions of Skopeo. But, to benefit from layer reuse and parallelism, using a registry-to-registry copy to a temporary registry, and transferring the backing storage of the temporary registry to the offline system, would be the most efficient way.

> After that, copying to oci: or oci-archive: would fail because those transports don’t currently implement that. 

… don’t implement storing signatures along with the image.

A possible, manual, alternative, would be to explicitly copy the signature object (`my-centos-2:sha256-eb4e282ef535e75f86245bedb32f51766b1b04d117a3f41cd63b33702ba2f362.sig`), separately.

But I’d still recommend using a registry-to-registry copy, or a dedicated mirroring tool like `oc-mirror`, instead.

The immediate cause of the command succeeding without any errors is missing configuration; that must be fixed by users.

Support for storing signatures in `oci:` is now tracked in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/186 .
